schema.appDescription: Calculate stamp duty and registration charges for property purchase in India. State-wise rates for Maharashtra, Karnataka, Delhi, UP, and other states.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Stamp Duty Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is stamp duty on property purchase in India?
faq.a: Stamp duty is a state tax on property transactions, typically 4–8% of the transaction value. It varies by state, gender (women buyers often get 1–2% concession), property type, and location (urban vs rural). It must be paid before registration. Non-payment makes the deed legally unenforceable.

faq.q: What are the stamp duty rates in Maharashtra 2026?
faq.a: Maharashtra stamp duty: 6% for properties above ₹30 lakh in urban areas (5% stamp duty + 1% metro cess). Women buyers get 1% rebate. Local body tax and LBT may apply. Registration fee: 1% of property value, capped at ₹30,000 for properties above ₹30 lakh.

Stamp duty and registration charges paid for a house are deductible under Section 80C (within ₹1.5 lakh limit) in the year of payment. This is in addition to the principal repayment on home loan. Only available under the old tax regime.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹50 lakh flat purchase in Mumbai
examples.text: Stamp duty (6%): ₹3,00,000. Registration charge (1%, capped at ₹30,000): ₹30,000. Total government charges: ₹3,30,000 — about 6.6% of property value. Women buyers pay 5% stamp duty instead of 6%, saving ₹50,000.

examples.heading: ₹1 crore property in Karnataka
examples.text: Karnataka stamp duty (5%): ₹5,00,000. Registration (1%): ₹1,00,000. Total: ₹6,00,000 on a ₹1 crore property. BBMP charges additional betterment levy in some areas. Always budget 6–8% over property cost for all charges.
